title: GNC Landing Simulation
description: Build a 2D simulation of a Falcon 9-style booster performing a propulsive landing. Implement guidance algorithms, thrust vector control, and a PID controller to land a simulated booster on a target pad.
whyItMatters: Propulsive landing is SpaceX's signature achievement. The guidance and control algorithms that steer a 25-tonne booster to a precision landing from Mach 6 represent some of the most sophisticated real-time control engineering in existence. This project teaches the fundamentals of that system.

steps.title: Build the dynamics model
steps.description: Implement 2D rigid-body dynamics for a rocket booster: position, velocity, attitude, angular rate. Include gravity, thrust (variable magnitude and direction), and aerodynamic drag.
steps.tips: Use state-space representation. The state vector is [x, y, vx, vy, theta, omega].

steps.title: Implement the guidance
steps.description: Code a powered descent guidance algorithm that computes the optimal thrust profile to reach the landing pad from the entry point. Start with a polynomial guidance law.
steps.tips: SpaceX uses convex optimisation for real-time powered descent guidance. A simplified version works for this project.

steps.title: Build the controller
steps.description: Implement a PID controller for thrust vector control that tracks the guidance commands. Tune gains for stability and response speed.
steps.tips: The engine can only gimbal a few degrees. Saturate the control input to model this constraint.

steps.title: Add disturbances
steps.description: Add wind gusts, sensor noise, and initial condition errors. Test the controller's robustness to these disturbances.
steps.tips: A good controller handles 10 m/s wind gusts without losing the landing pad.

steps.title: Visualise the landing
steps.description: Animate the landing sequence in real-time. Show the booster trajectory, thrust vector, and landing pad. Record success/failure rates over many runs.
steps.tips: Use Pygame or matplotlib animation. Show the trajectory trace and current attitude.

deliverables: Robustness study with Monte Carlo results
Comparison with published Falcon 9 landing parameters

rubric.description: Physics model produces realistic trajectories with correct scale and timing
Guidance algorithm generates fuel-efficient trajectories to the landing point
Controller successfully lands the booster in 90%+ of Monte Carlo runs
Clear, informative animation that shows the control system working
